source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — 1912, copyright expired)
title: Russian Church, Kodiak, Alaska. After a 16 in. fall of ashes from Katmai volcano
description: June 23, 1912 photograph of the Russian Orthodox church in Kodiak after the catastrophic eruption of Novarupta/Mount Katmai (June 6-8, 1912) deposited roughly 16 inches of volcanic ash on the town — the largest volcanic eruption of the 20th century. The pond in the foreground, previously filled with water at this spot, is now filled completely with ash. Residents evacuated to the cutter Manning in the harbor for days while the ash blotted out the sun.
alt: June 1912 black-and-white photograph of the Russian Orthodox church in Kodiak coated with volcanic ash after the Novarupta Katmai eruption, with an ash-filled pond in the foreground
